# Libya NDC Transformer Mapping Report

**Generated by**: AGENT 1 (strategy_builder)  
**Date**: 2026-04-07  
**Source**: `sisepuede/attributes/attribute_transformer_code.csv` (73 transformers confirmed)

---

## Valid Transformer Inventory Summary

All transformer codes are drawn from the canonical attribute table at:
`/Users/fabianfuentes/git/sisepuede/sisepuede/attributes/attribute_transformer_code.csv`

The `transformer_code` column uses the pattern `TFR:{SUBSECTOR}:{ACTION}`. These are the authoritative IDs passed to `Transformation` objects via config.

**Total transformers in attribute table**: 73 (IDs 0–72)

**Sectors covered**: AF (AFOLU), CE (Circular Economy), EN (Energy), IP (IPPU), CROSS

---

## NDC Axis to Transformer Mapping

| NDC Axis | Description | Matched transformer_code | Sector | Subsector | Strategy | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Gas Flaring Recovery | `TFR:FGTV:DEC_LEAKS` | EN | FGTV | S1, S2 | Reduces CO2/CH4/N2O leaks from oil & gas fugitive emissions |
| 1 | Gas Flaring Recovery (complement) | `TFR:FGTV:INC_FLARE` | EN | FGTV | S1, S2 | Converts vented gas to flared (less potent GHG than raw CH4 venting) |
| 2 | Tree Planting 100M trees | `TFR:LNDU:INC_REFORESTATION` | AF | LNDU | S1 (scalar 0.1), S2 (scalar 1.0) | Increases probability of land transitioning to secondary forest. Scalar 0.1 represents ~10% of full target (unconditional 10M trees). Full scalar 1.0 = 100M tree conditional target |
| 3 | Electricity Demand Efficiency | `TFR:SCOE:INC_EFFICIENCY_APPLIANCE` | EN | SCOE | S1, S2 | Fractional improvement in electrical appliance efficiency in residential and commercial buildings |
| 4 | Thermal Energy Demand | `TFR:SCOE:DEC_DEMAND_HEAT` | EN | SCOE | S2 | Reduce heat demand by improving building shell; conditional on building retrofit programs |
| 5 | Power Generation Efficiency | `TFR:ENTC:DEC_LOSSES` | EN | ENTC | S1, S2 | Reduce transmission/distribution losses by upgrading electrical infrastructure |
| 6 | Renewables in Power Generation | `TFR:ENTC:TARGET_RENEWABLE_ELEC` | EN | ENTC | S2 | Target 95% renewable electricity by final time period; conditional on investment |
| 7 | Cement Processes | `TFR:IPPU:DEC_CLINKER` | IP | IPPU | S2 | Reduce clinker-to-cement ratio; addresses process CO2 from calcination |
| 8 | Cement Energy | `TFR:INEN:INC_EFFICIENCY_ENERGY` | EN | INEN | S1, S2 | Maximize industrial energy efficiency; applies to cement and other industries |
| 9 | Iron & Steel | `TFR:INEN:INC_EFFICIENCY_PRODUCTION` | EN | INEN | S2 | Maximize production efficiency (kJ/tonne); conditional on modernization investment |
| 10 | HFCs | `TFR:IPPU:DEC_HFCS` | IP | IPPU | S2 | Reduce HFC emission factors per unit production; covers refrigerant phase-down aligned with Kigali Amendment |
| 11 | Solid Waste | `TFR:WASO:INC_RECYCLING` | CE | WASO | S2 | Increase recycling fraction, reducing waste sent to landfills and associated CH4 |
| 11 | Solid Waste (biogas) | `TFR:WASO:INC_CAPTURE_BIOGAS` | CE | WASO | S2 | Increase biogas capture fraction at landfills and anaerobic digesters |
| 12 | Wastewater | `TFR:WALI:INC_TREATMENT_URBAN` | CE | WALI | S2 | Improve urban wastewater treatment pathways; relevant to Libyan urban centers |
| 12 | Wastewater (biogas capture) | `TFR:TRWW:INC_CAPTURE_BIOGAS` | CE | TRWW | S2 | Increase biogas capture at anaerobic wastewater treatment facilities |
| 13 | Agriculture | `TFR:LVST:DEC_ENTERIC_FERMENTATION` | AF | LVST | S2 | Reduce enteric fermentation (CH4) from cattle and small ruminants |
| 13 | Agriculture (soils) | `TFR:SOIL:DEC_N_APPLIED` | AF | SOIL | S2 | Reduce nitrogen application, lowering N2O from agricultural soils |
| 14 | Transport | `TFR:TRNS:INC_EFFICIENCY_NON_ELECTRIC` | EN | TRNS | S1, S2 | Increase non-electric vehicle fuel efficiency; primary mode in Libya |
| 14 | Transport (electrification) | `TFR:TRNS:SHIFT_FUEL_LIGHT_DUTY` | EN | TRNS | S2 | Electrify light-duty vehicles; conditional on renewable grid |

---

## NDC Axes with No Direct Transformer Match

None. All 14 NDC axes have at least one matched transformer. However, two axes have nuanced coverage:

**NDC Axis 4 (Thermal Energy Demand)**: Only `TFR:SCOE:DEC_DEMAND_HEAT` available (building shell insulation). The companion fuel switch `TFR:SCOE:SHIFT_FUEL_HEAT` was considered but excluded from S1 since Libya's unconditional commitment does not include electrification of space heating — grid is not yet clean enough to justify this as unconditional.

**NDC Axis 9 (Iron & Steel)**: Libya has limited iron & steel production. `TFR:INEN:INC_EFFICIENCY_PRODUCTION` is used as the closest match (production efficiency). No steel-specific transformer exists; the INEN module treats all heavy industry together. This is explicitly conditional.

---

## Transformers NOT Included (with rationale)

| transformer_code | Rationale for exclusion |
|---|---|
| `TFR:AGRC:DEC_CH4_RICE` | Libya has negligible rice production; not relevant to NDC |
| `TFR:LNDU:DEC_DEFORESTATION` | Libya has minimal forest cover; deforestation not a significant source |
| `TFR:ENTC:LEAST_COST_SOLUTION` | Model operation transformer, not a policy intervention |
| `TFR:CCSQ:INC_CAPTURE` | Libya's NDC does not include direct air capture; no CCS commitment |
| `TFR:PFLO:INC_IND_CCS` | No industrial CCS in Libya NDC |
| `TFR:ENFU:ADJ_EXPORTS` | Fuel export adjustment is economic policy, not mitigation |
| `TFR:LNDU:DEC_SOC_LOSS_PASTURES` | Covered implicitly by reforestation; not in Libya NDC |
| `TFR:TRNS:SHIFT_MODE_PASSENGER` | Mode shift to public transit not in Libya NDC |
| `TFR:WASO:INC_ANAEROBIC_AND_COMPOST` | Selected recycling + biogas capture as more direct NDC match |

## Strategy Summary

| Strategy | ID | Name | Transformer Count | NDC Axes Covered |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Strategy 0 | 0 | Baseline | 0 | None |
| Strategy 1 | 1 | Unconditional NDC | 7 | 1, 2 (partial), 3, 5, 8, 14 |
| Strategy 2 | 2 | Conditional NDC | 19 | 1–14 (all) |

---

## Key Decisions

1. Gas Flaring Recovery uses TWO transformers (`TFR:FGTV:DEC_LEAKS` + `TFR:FGTV:INC_FLARE`) together — both are needed to capture the full NDC intent: minimizing methane venting and converting remaining vented gas to CO2 via flaring.

2. Tree Planting scalar of 0.1 for Strategy 1 represents the unconditional 10M tree commitment out of the conditional 100M tree target. SISEPUEDE's `TFR:LNDU:INC_REFORESTATION` magnitude parameter should be calibrated against Libya's land area baseline.

3. Iron & Steel mapped to `TFR:INEN:INC_EFFICIENCY_PRODUCTION` (production efficiency, kJ/tonne) rather than a fuel switch, since Libya's steel sector is nascent and efficiency improvements are more appropriate than fuel switching at this stage.

4. Wastewater NDC axis covered by both WALI (treatment pathway) and TRWW (biogas capture) to reflect Libya's goals of expanding treatment infrastructure AND capturing methane from existing anaerobic facilities.
